name: send
context: Send a native ETH transaction on any EVM chain with explicit fee controls and receipt handling
info: Broadcasts a native ETH transfer using a JSON-RPC endpoint plus a sender private key. Supports legacy `gasPrice` and EIP-1559 fee fields, optional nonce override, optional calldata, automatic gas estimation with a safety buffer, dry-run preparation, and optional waiting for the mined receipt. This is the simplest real transaction path an agent can execute on Ethereum, Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, or other EVM-compatible chains.
body:
# Send Native ETH

Use this skill when you need to move native ETH from one wallet to another on an EVM chain. It is intentionally narrower than a general contract-call skill: fewer moving parts means better reliability for agents.

Inputs can come from the request payload or environment variables. `RPC_URL` and `PRIVATE_KEY` are the only hard requirements. The value can be supplied as `value_eth` for human readability or `value_wei` when precision matters.

The script estimates gas when `gas_limit` is omitted and adds a small safety buffer. If you supply `max_fee_per_gas_gwei` or `max_priority_fee_per_gas_gwei`, it sends an EIP-1559 transaction. Otherwise it falls back to legacy `gas_price_gwei` when provided, or lets the provider fill fee data. Set `dry_run` when you need to inspect the prepared request before broadcast.

Before using this in production, read [[ethereum/gas#eip-1559]] and [[ethereum/transactions#nonce-discipline]]. If you are transferring tokens rather than native ETH, switch to [[ethereum/erc20]].

sections.title: Value units
sections.body: Prefer `value_wei` when an upstream system already computed the exact amount. Use `value_eth` for simple human-entered transfers like `0.01`. Do not send both unless you deliberately want `value_wei` to win.

sections.title: Fee fields
sections.body: If you provide `max_fee_per_gas_gwei` or `max_priority_fee_per_gas_gwei`, the script builds an EIP-1559 transaction. If you provide only `gas_price_gwei`, it uses legacy pricing. If you provide none of them, the provider fills fee data from the chain.

sections.title: Receipt handling
sections.body: Set `wait_for_receipt` to `false` when you only need the submitted hash and will watch confirmation elsewhere. Leave it `true` when the caller needs block number, gas used, and final success status in one response.

sections.title: Dry run mode
sections.body: Set `dry_run` to `true` to inspect the prepared transaction request without broadcasting. This is useful when an agent needs to surface fees, nonce, or exact value before asking for final approval.

scripts.name: Send Native ETH Transaction
scripts.description: Send native ETH to a recipient and optionally wait for the receipt

RPC_URL.description: JSON-RPC endpoint for the target chain

rpc_url.description: JSON-RPC endpoint for the target chain

value_eth.description: ETH amount as a decimal string

value_wei.description: ETH amount in wei; overrides value_eth when present

gas_limit_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the gas estimate

max_fee_per_gas_gwei.description: EIP-1559 max fee per gas in gwei

max_priority_fee_per_gas_gwei.description: EIP-1559 max priority fee in gwei

confirmations.description: Receipt confirmation count to wait for

depends.reason: Fee selection and estimation are part of every send flow

links.context: Fee model for max fee and priority fee fields
Nonce handling when multiple transactions may be pending
Switch here for token transfers rather than native ETH

related.note: Use ERC-20 tooling when the asset is a token contract, not native ETH
Fee reasoning matters before broadcasting to mainnet

author: dojo-community
